description: Feature 11: Implement retranslation control - allow users to decide whether existing translations in an STF/Excel file should be kept or retranslated. Add CLI flag --retranslate-existing. Provide row-level retranslation control in Phase 4 review. Modify the runner to support a 'retranslate_existing' mode that clears existing translations before sending to backend.
steps: Modify src/stx/translate/runner.py: add retranslate_existing: bool = False parameter to translate_document() and _Runner.__init__(). When True, the _classify() method should NOT skip rows that already have translations (remove the 'already translated' skip logic for those rows). Track retranslated_count in TranslationResult.

Modify src/stx/cli.py: add --retranslate-existing flag (bool, default False) to the translate and run commands. When set, pass retranslate_existing=True to translate_document(). Print info about retranslating existing translations in the output summary.

Add to src/stx/gui/pages/phase3_translate.py: In the setup section (or as a dialog on start), add a choice when the document has both translated and untranslated entries: 'Use existing translations' (default, checkbox checked) vs 'Retranslate existing translations' (checkbox unchecked). This controls whether retranslate_existing=True is passed to the worker. Only show this choice if the document has translated entries.
Modify src/stx/gui/workers.py TranslationWorker to accept and pass retranslate_existing parameter to translate_document()

Modify src/stx/gui/pages/phase4_review.py: Add row-level retranslation - when a user clears the translation of a specific row (sets it to empty string) and there is a connected translation worker capability, mark it for retranslation. Implement a 'Retranslate row' context menu action or button that clears a row's translation and triggers a single-row retranslation on demand. For simplicity, clearing a translation and then running translate again will retranslate that row since it becomes untranslated.
Add retranslate_existing field to src/stx/gui/state.py AppState: retranslate_existing: bool = False

Create tests/test_retranslate.py with tests covering: (a) retranslate_existing=False skips already-translated rows (existing behavior), (b) retranslate_existing=True retranslates already-translated rows, (c) interaction with imported_translations (imported still takes priority even when retranslating), (d) TranslationResult tracks retranslated_count correctly, (e) row-level clear triggers retranslation on next run
acceptance_criteria: translate_document(retranslate_existing=True) retranslates rows that already have translations
CLI --retranslate-existing flag works with translate and run commands
GUI Phase 3 shows a retranslation choice when document has existing translations
Phase 4 has a way to clear a row translation for retranslation on next run
Imported translations still take highest priority even with retranslate_existing=True
findings: All 343 tests pass (329 existing + 14 new). The retranslate_existing parameter is threaded through translate_document, translate_document_multi, _Runner, CLI (translate and run commands), GUI workers, and Phase 3 page. Imported translations still take highest priority even with retranslate_existing=True. The retranslated_count field tracks how many rows with pre-existing translations were retranslated. Phase 4 has a context menu action 'Clear for retranslation' that sets translation to empty string.
